deviations.what: The original model value is a JS Date object (UI5Date.getInstance()) and every TimeType binding has NO source formatOption (the type's default model format is a Date object). abap2UI5 cannot hold a JS Date object in the JSON model, so a source formatOption { source: { pattern: 'HH:mm:ss' } } is added to each TimeType binding and the field is a parseable time string ('13:30:00') - the abap2UI5 equivalent of the Date-object model. Without it view creation crashes ('Time must be a Date object'). Same idiom applies to any TypeTime/TypeDateTime sample whose model is an object; a plain string binding is not enough (CAPABILITIES date-object row).
The original seeds the current time; a fixed time (13:30:00) is used here so the port is deterministic - a client-only display value.
